flashcards.q: Define 'Resource' in the 3-criteria context.
flashcards.a: Technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable.

flashcards.q: What are Biotic resources?
flashcards.a: Resources obtained from the biosphere that have life (flora, fauna, humans).

flashcards.q: Give examples of Abiotic resources.
What is the territorial water limit for national ownership in India?
flashcards.a: 12 nautical miles (22.2 km) from the coast.

flashcards.q: How far does the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) extend?
Where was the 1992 Earth Summit held?
What is the main goal of Agenda 21?
flashcards.a: To achieve global sustainable development and combat environmental damage and poverty.

flashcards.q: Define Sustainable Development.
flashcards.a: Development without damaging the environment or compromising future generations' needs.

flashcards.q: In which book did Schumacher present Gandhian philosophy in 1974?
Which report introduced 'Sustainable Development' in 1987?
What percentage of India's land is Plains?
What percentage of India is Mountains?
What is the share of Plateaus in India's land features?
What is the total geographical area of India?
Why is land use data only available for 93% of India?
flashcards.a: Reporting for some NE states (except Assam) and J&K areas (PoK/China) is incomplete.

flashcards.q: What is the target forest percentage in the 1952 policy?
What is the actual forest cover in India (~2019-20)?
What causes severe land degradation in Jharkhand and Odisha?
What is the main cause of degradation in Gujarat and Rajasthan?
Why is land degrading in Punjab and Haryana?
flashcards.a: Over-irrigation leading to waterlogging and salinity.

flashcards.q: How is soil formed?
flashcards.a: By forces of nature like temperature changes, water, wind, and decomposers over millions of years.

flashcards.q: What is 'Regur' soil?
Distinguish Bangar and Khadar.
flashcards.a: Bangar: Old alluvial (high kanker); Khadar: New alluvial (fine-grained, fertile).

flashcards.q: Which soil type result from intense leaching?
Latin word 'later' means?
Which soil is best for growing cashew nuts in TN/Kerala?
What restricts water infiltration in arid soils?
flashcards.a: Presence of Kankar (calcium content) in lower horizons.

flashcards.q: Where are Forest soils acidic and humus-poor?
flashcards.a: In snow-covered areas of the Himalayas.

flashcards.q: What is 'Gully Erosion'?
flashcards.a: Running water cutting deep channels in clayey soils.

flashcards.q: What are 'Ravines'?
flashcards.a: Badlands created by gully erosion, common in the Chambal basin.

flashcards.q: What is 'Sheet Erosion'?
flashcards.a: Washing away of the topsoil layer over large areas down a slope.

flashcards.q: Define 'Contour Ploughing'.
flashcards.a: Ploughing along contour lines to decelerate water flow down slopes.

flashcards.q: Where is Terrace farming well-developed in India?
How does 'Strip Cropping' prevent erosion?
flashcards.a: Grass strips between crops break the force of the wind.

flashcards.q: What are 'Shelter Belts'?
flashcards.a: Lines of trees planted to stabilize sand dunes in deserts.

flashcards.q: What is 'Net Sown Area'?
flashcards.a: Physical land sown/harvested at least once in an agricultural year.

flashcards.q: What is 'Gross Cropped Area'?
flashcards.a: Net Sown Area plus area sown more than once in a year.

flashcards.q: Which relief feature ensures perennial flow of rivers?
What did Gandhiji blame for resource depletion?
flashcards.a: Greed of individuals and the exploitative nature of modern technology.

flashcards.q: Who advocated resource conservation first in 1968?
Soil grains become coarser as one moves...?
Fine particles are more common in which alluvial plains?
Which soil turns yellow in hydrated form?
Why is the kankar layer useful in some contexts?
flashcards.a: It indicates calcium content but restricts water infiltration.

flashcards.q: Land use is determined by which 2 sets of factors?
